title: Test confirmation protocol across 3+ simulated nodes
markdown:
## Goal

Create a comprehensive integration test suite that simulates 3 nodes running the full confirmation protocol: submission, pending marker, discovery, attestation, threshold check, and finalization. This validates that the entire confirmation lifecycle works end-to-end with realistic node interactions.

## Background

Individual components of the confirmation protocol are tested in their respective stories (G2-A2-01 through G2-A2-08). This story creates the integration test that wires them all together:

1. Three MemoryClient instances represent three nodes (Node A, B, C)
2. Peer replication is configured so writes propagate between them
3. A client submits a consensus envelope to Node A
4. Node A writes the pending marker
5. Node B and Node C discover the pending marker (via replicated data)
6. Each node replays validation and writes attestations
7. When threshold (2) is met, one node writes the confirmation
8. The client's `waitForConfirmation()` succeeds

This test validates the correctness of the distributed consensus flow without requiring actual network infrastructure.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Integration test creates 3 simulated nodes with peer replication
- [ ] Test configures `VALIDATOR_SET` with the 3 node keys and `CONFIRMATION_THRESHOLD = 2`
- [ ] Test submits a valid consensus envelope (genesis + balance + fee + consensus record)
- [ ] Test verifies pending marker is created and replicated
- [ ] Test runs discovery loops on all 3 nodes
- [ ] Test verifies at least 2 attestations are written
- [ ] Test verifies confirmation is written after threshold is met
- [ ] Test verifies `consensus://record` is written after confirmation
- [ ] Test verifies `waitForConfirmation()` returns true
- [ ] Test verifies the finalization race (only one confirmation write succeeds)
- [ ] Test runs in under 10 seconds (no real network, no real delays)
- [ ] Negative test: submission with invalid conservation is rejected by all nodes

## Implementation Guide

1. Create the test infrastructure:
   ```typescript
   // Three nodes with shared schema
   const schema = firecatSchema; // With confirmation validators registered

   const nodeAStorage = new MemoryClient({ schema });
   const nodeBStorage = new MemoryClient({ schema });
   const nodeCStorage = new MemoryClient({ schema });

   // Each node pushes to the other two
   const nodeA = createValidatedClient({
     write: parallelBroadcast([nodeAStorage, bestEffortClient(nodeBStorage), bestEffortClient(nodeCStorage)]),
     read: firstMatchSequence([nodeAStorage]),
     validate: msgSchema(schema),
   });
   // Similar for nodeB, nodeC
   ```

2. Generate three node identities (signing key pairs).

3. Configure the validator set:
   ```typescript
   // Temporarily set VALIDATOR_SET for this test
   VALIDATOR_SET.push(nodeAKey, nodeBKey, nodeCKey);
   ```

4. Build and submit a genesis + consensus envelope:
   ```typescript
   // Genesis claim for test account
   const genesisEnvelope = buildGenesisEnvelope(testPubKey, 1000);
   await nodeA.receive(["hash://sha256/" + envelopeHash, genesisEnvelope]);
   // ... then build consensus envelope with gas payment
   ```

5. Run the pending processor on Node A.

6. Manually trigger discovery loop polls on each node.

7. Verify attestation count reaches threshold.

8. Trigger finalization.

9. Verify end state: confirmation exists, consensus record exists.

## Files to Create/Modify

- `libs/firecat-protocol/confirmation_test.ts` -- Add multi-node integration test.
- `libs/firecat-protocol/confirmation.ts` -- Fix any issues discovered during integration testing.

## Testing

```bash
deno test libs/firecat-protocol/confirmation_test.ts
```

The test should be self-contained with no external dependencies.

## Dependencies

Depends on G2-A2-07 (finalization), G2-A2-08 (waitForConfirmation), G2-E1-01 (peer replication hardening). Blocks G2-E1-04 (staging environment is the real-world version of this test).
tasksExcerpt:
- [ ] Test and harden peer replication across multiple b3nd-nodes
- [ ] Test confirmation protocol across 3+ nodes
- [ ] Implement managed-node improvements (node builder, config watcher, heartbeat — libs exist but integration unclear)
- [ ] Set up multi-node staging environment
